sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking the purple ring at (41, 49) didn't change much - the board looks the same. This is different from level 1 where clicking the ring created a path.
Looking at the current state:
- White diamond (W, 12px) at (6, 17) with orange child - already has a path
- Orange ring (O, 20px) at (12, 24) with magenta child
- Purple ring (p, 20px) at (41, 49) with magenta child
- Dark gray boxes at (9, 49), (21, 8), (35, 45), (48, 54)
- Orange diamond at (48, 39)
The white diamond at (6, 17) already has a path. Looking at the image, I can see:
1. Top-left: White diamond with path going to dark gray at (21, 8)
2. Top-right: Orange ring at (12, 24) with path going to dark gray at (9, 49)
3. Bottom-right: Purple ring at (41, 49) with path going to dark gray at (48, 54)
4. Bottom-middle: Orange diamond at (48, 39) with path going to dark gray at (35, 45)
The white diamond is already at the start. I need to advance it along its path. Looking at the path from (6, 17) to (21, 8), there are light gray (w) waypoints.
Let me try clicking the white diamond at (6, 17) to advance it along its path.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White diamond at (6,17) already has a path. Try clicking it to advance.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 6, 'col': 17}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After white diamond click:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] < 30 and n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'p', 'O', 'M', 'w']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
